channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Mission and the Quest for Lunar Resources
item.description: NASA's upcoming Artemis II mission will return astronauts to the Moon in 2026, but before that, two scouting missions will explore the lunar south pole for resources that could sustain human space travel and enable new scientific discoveries.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Fubo merging with Disney’s Hulu + Live TV
item.description: Combining the services should attract subscribers as customers look for online alternatives to cable TV.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: New HP Laptops: EliteBook X, Flip, and Ultra Models Unveiled with Intel Core Ultra CPUs
item.description: HP introduces three new 14-inch laptops, the EliteBook X G1i, EliteBook X Flip G1i, and EliteBook Ultra G1i, all powered by Intel's latest mobile Core Ultra CPUs.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Dell launches newly rebranded laptops at CES 2025 to replace storied XPS, Inspiron, and other product lines
item.description: Dell announced a shakeup of its entire laptop portfolio at CES 2025, retiring decades-old product brands for a more 'simplified' structure.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: Tesla data helped police after Las Vegas truck explosion, but experts have wider privacy concerns
item.description: Privacy data experts say the deep dive by Elon Musk’s company was impressive, but also shines a spotlight on a difficult question as vehicles become less like cars and more like computers on wheels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China rips US over sanctions in hacking incidents targeting infrastructure
item.description: China slammed the U.S. Monday for sanctioning a Beijing-based cybersecurity firm allegedly behind a botnet targeting American infrastructure, accusing Washington of “using the issue of cybersecurity to vilify and smear China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Salt Typhoon Cyberattacks Expose More Telecoms Providers - WSJ Report
item.description: US telecoms companies Charter Communications, Consolidated Communications, and Windstream affected by Salt Typhoon cyberattack - WSJ report reveals fresh list of victims.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Self-healing AI PCs with smart agents are coming, and no, please don't call them Skynet
item.description: AI PCs with NPUs deliver predictive power, fix issues before you notice them, cut costs and boost enterprise productivity
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Artificial intelligence spots a wildfire in a remote California canyon – and promptly alerts firefighters
item.description: It was the first time the Orange County Fire Authority got pinged by AI about a vegetation fire without receiving a 911 call from a human being, the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Kalfus, The Mercury News

item.title: Demand for AI skills soars, while demand for programming skills falls – O’Reilly report
item.description: It’s perhaps not a surprise, but demand for AI-related skills has soared, according to technology educator O’Reilly, which just released its annual technology trends report. By contrast, interest in programming languages Python and Java has taken a bit of a nosedive, the company reports.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft embarrasses itself with Windows 10 pop-up that hogs the desktop urging an upgrade to Windows 11 – then promptly crashes
item.description: Windows 10 users, start planning for Windows 11 now – by watching Microsoft’s pop-up pushing an upgrade crash.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What to know about the Meta glasses the New Orleans attacker used to scout the French Quarter
item.description: The FBI says the New Orleans truck attacker who killed 14 people had previously scouted the French Quarter and recorded video with his Meta smart glasses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What is an EPMO? Your organization’s strategy navigator
item.description: An enterprise-focused PMO aligns every initiative with your organization’s strategic goals, ensuring that the right projects deliver the right results at the right time.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What to expect from NaaS in 2025
item.description: Enterprises have been slow to adopt NaaS for a number of reasons, including lack of clarity on exactly what NaaS is, lack of a solid business case that NaaS saves money over the long term, and questions around security, visibility, control, and data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How Big Tech became the world’s most powerful ‘religion’ and why we need to become agnostic
item.description: Greg Epstein is the Humanist chaplain at Harvard University and at MIT, where he advises students, faculty, and staff members on ethical and existential concerns from a humanist perspective.
item.dc:creator: Next Big Idea Club, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: It took police more than a week to publicly identify 57-year-old Debrina Kawam as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Goldin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: The ruling hands a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden admin officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ruby completes switch to Prism parser
item.description: Ruby 3.4.0, and Ruby 3.4.1, updates to the longstanding dynamic, open source programming language, have arrived, with Prism now the default parser. Ruby 3.4.0 was released December 25, Christmas Day, along with an update, Ruby 3.4.1, which merely changed the version number of the release. Ruby 3.4.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Graviton progress: 50% of new AWS instances run on Amazon custom silicon
item.description: Half of all instances spun up on Amazon Web Services over the past two years used Graviton, Amazon’s custom silicon built on Arm processor technology, rather than x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI still hasn’t released tools to deny data collection
item.description: OpenAI has failed to release the tool to opt-out or customize data collection the company promised to make available by 2025, according to Techcrunch.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Confronting the growing drone threat
item.description: There is a solid rationale for prioritizing safe, secure and controlled RF cyber-based counter-UAS technology as decision makers consider future standards. The post Confronting the growing drone threat first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Nearly a year since launch, Apple’s Vision Pro still searching for a killer app
item.description: Apple launched its highly anticipated Vision Pro “spatial computing” headset last Feb. 2 amid significant hype — and hopes it could finally push virtual or mixed reality into mainstream use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The biggest Wi-Fi mistake you didn’t know you were making
item.description: There are many untapped safeguards and tips you can learn to take your Wi-Fi knowledge to the next level, technology expert Kim Komando shares her tricks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
